channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Kevin Warsh's real Fed 'regime change' may happen deep inside Wall Street's plumbing
item.description: Warsh could guide to a smaller role in day-to-day markets, while also setting clearer rules for how and when it should intervene.

item.title: SpaceX launches Starship test flight on second try
item.description: SpaceX launched its massive Starship rocket on Friday, a day after the company scrubbed plans before takeoff.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigning as Trump's intelligence chief
item.description: Gabbard said she is leaving her post to support her husband, who is battling a rare form of bone cancer.

item.title: Magnitude 6 earthquake strikes Hawaii’s Big Island; USGS assessing Kilauea volcano
item.description: Kilauea, one of the world's most active volcanoes, is located on Hawaii's Big Island.

item.title: Three signs from APEC that the U.S. and China remain far apart on trade
item.description: U.S. and Chinese officials have met and spoken publicly about differing priorities since the Trump-Xi summit concluded in Beijing last week.

item.title: New lawsuits against Trump's DOJ 'lawfare' fund
item.description: President Trump droppped his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS in exchange for the DOJ creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate alleged "lawfare" victims.

item.title: Dow adds nearly 300 points Friday for new record close; S&P 500 notches eighth winning week: Live updates
item.description: Volatility picked up this week in the stock and bond markets.

item.title: Trump skipping wedding of son Donald Jr. to Bettina Anderson
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. was previously married to Vanessa Trump, with whom he has five children. She disclosed this week that she has breast cancer.

item.title: Qualcomm's stock pop shows investors are 'waking up' to boom in AI devices
item.description: Qualcomm is on a big run, powered by investor bullishness that the company will be at the center of a boom in AI devices.

item.title: Wall Street thinks IMAX is ripe for a sale. Here's who could buy it
item.description: A source told CNBC that IMAX has held "preliminary talks" through intermediaries, but no official pitches have been made by the company.

item.title: Disney's 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' tallies lowest Thursday preview sales in franchise history
item.description: Disney's "Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied $12 million in Thursday night previews, the lowest collection of advance tickets in franchise history.

item.title: Donald Trump Jr.-backed 'Steroid Olympics' are happening on Sunday. Here's what to expect
item.description: Olympic medallists are among 42 athletes competing in the controversial competition, but the Enhanced Games has big product plans beyond the event this weekend.

item.title: Two top Walmart executives leave company under new CEO John Furner
item.description: Tom Ward, chief operating officer of Walmart's warehouse chain Sam's Club, is retiring, and Cedric Clark, Walmart's EVP of U.S. store operations, is leaving.

item.title: Judge tosses Kilmar Abrego Garcia charges, calls Trump administration prosecution 'vindictive'
item.description: A judge found Trump’s DOJ targeted Kilmar Abrego Garcia after he challenged his mistaken deportation to El Salvador.

item.title: Here's the case for 9,000 on the S&P 500 by the middle of next year, according to JPMorgan
item.description: Higher productivity could be even more supportive of the stock market than previously thought.

item.title: Consumer sentiment hits fresh record low in May as Iran war fuels inflation worries
item.description: Consumer sentiment has tumbled to a fresh record low in May as fears of higher prices grow due to the U.S.-Iran war and elevated oil prices.

item.title: Trump said he'd 'remember' companies that didn't apply for tariff refunds. Many of them are anyway
item.description: Walmart confirmed to CNBC it has sought a tariff refund and plans to use any money it receives back to invest in lower prices for shoppers.

item.title: Stocks continue surging to record highs. Here's how to hedge
item.description: The rally back to records has made buying protection more affordable.

item.title: Oil-rich Alberta to hold a vote on whether to separate from Canada
item.description: The move for a referendum comes after months of campaigning from a group of separatists.

item.title: Egg prices are plunging due to oversupply — and producers say margins are taking a hit as costs rise
item.description: Egg prices are plunging from oversupply after avian flu shortages, but producers say persistent feed, fuel and labor costs are squeezing margins.

item.title: Arm shares extend weekly rally to almost 50%, and Starbucks pulls the plug on an AI project
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: What is a tax relief company?
item.description: How tax relief companies work, what they can (and can’t) do, and what to watch out for.

item.title: Despite murky legal landscape, companies are undeterred in their prediction market investments
item.description: Companies reiterated plans to growing their prediction markets businesses in recent earnings calls even as a regulatory debate continues.

item.title: U.S., Iran signal peace progress — but remain at odds over enriched uranium, Strait of Hormuz tolls
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday said there were "good signs" that an agreement to end the Middle East conflict is in sight.

item.title: Which company will the U.S. government take a stake in next? Here’s what traders think
item.description: After new stakes in quantum companies were revealed, traders on prediction market platform Kalshi think IonQ, Micron and Anduril could be next.

item.title: Surge in 'risk-free' treasury yields sends bond investors in search of better opportunities
item.description: Treasury yield surge shows bond market is not 'risk free' after all, but there's opportunity for fixed-income investors in intermediates, BBBs and high yield.

item.title: Iran war leaves U.S. gas prices at highest levels in nearly four years ahead of Memorial Day
item.description: Global oil inventories are declining fast with just four to six weeks until gasoline, diesel and jet fuel prices shoot higher, analysts said.

item.title: What the AvalonBay, Equity Residential megamerger means for the apartment industry and rents
item.description: The deal will create one of the largest real estate companies in the U.S., with more than 180,000 rental apartments.

item.title: Oversight Chairman Comer launches congressional probe into insider trading on Kalshi, Polymarket
item.description: Congress is ramping up pressure on prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket over concerns of insider trading.

item.title: Microsoft’s GitHub was positioned to win the AI coding race. Outages got in the way
item.description: GitHub's user base has swelled under Microsoft's ownership, but the software repository has fallen behind newer rivals in the world of vibe coding.
